Freelancer & Contractor Agreement Generator

Generate a comprehensive independent contractor agreement covering scope of work, payment terms, intellectual property ownership, confidentiality, termination clauses, and liability limitations — written in plain English that both parties can actually understand.

Beginner PASTE & GO Risk-reduction
Pro tip

AI-generated contracts are starting points, NOT legal advice. Always have a lawyer review before signing. That said, a well-structured draft saves you 80% of legal fees because your lawyer is editing, not writing from scratch.

contract freelancer contractor agreement legal ip ownership

Prompt Variants by Model

Claude Claude 4.x
FRESH APR 2026 
You are a business lawyer drafting an independent contractor agreement in plain English. This is a STARTING POINT for legal review, not final legal advice.

<engagement_details>
Hiring company: [YOUR...
You are a business lawyer drafting an independent contractor agreement in plain English. This is a STARTING POINT for legal review, not final legal advice.

<engagement_details>
Hiring company: [YOUR...

Enter your email to unlock this prompt

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

You are a business lawyer drafting an independent contractor agreement in plain English. This is a STARTING POINT for legal review, not final legal advice.

<engagement_details>
Hiring company: [YOUR BUSINESS NAME, LOCATION]
Contractor: [CONTRACTOR NAME OR "TBD"]
Project description: [WHAT THEY WILL DO — be specific]
Deliverables: [WHAT YOU EXPECT TO RECEIVE — list specific items]
Timeline: [START DATE, END DATE OR ONGOING, KEY MILESTONES]
Payment: [TOTAL AMOUNT OR HOURLY RATE, PAYMENT SCHEDULE, PAYMENT METHOD]
</engagement_details>

<special_requirements>
IP ownership: [WHO OWNS THE WORK — typically the hiring company for work-for-hire]
Confidentiality needs: [WHAT SENSITIVE INFO WILL THEY ACCESS]
Non-compete: [ANY RESTRICTIONS ON WORKING WITH COMPETITORS — note: enforceability varies by state]
Revision rounds: [HOW MANY REVISIONS ARE INCLUDED]
Tools and expenses: [WHO PROVIDES TOOLS, WHO PAYS EXPENSES]
</special_requirements>

Draft an agreement with these sections:

1. **Parties and effective date**
2. **Scope of work** — specific deliverables, timeline, milestones
3. **Compensation** — amount, schedule, invoicing process, late payment terms
4. **Independent contractor status** — clarify they are NOT an employee (tax implications, no benefits)
5. **Intellectual property** — work product ownership, pre-existing IP carve-out, license grants
6. **Confidentiality** — what is covered, duration (typically 2 years post-termination), exceptions
7. **Revision and approval process** — included rounds, additional revision rates
8. **Termination** — how either party can end the agreement, notice period, payment for completed work
9. **Liability and indemnification** — limitation of liability, mutual indemnification
10. **Dispute resolution** — governing law, mediation before litigation
11. **General provisions** — entire agreement, amendments, assignment, severability
12. **Signature blocks**

Write in plain English. Avoid unnecessary legalese. Where a legal term is necessary, include a parenthetical explanation.

End with a disclaimer: "This document was generated by AI as a starting point. Have it reviewed by a qualified attorney before signing."
Notes: Claude produces well-structured legal documents. The plain-English approach with parenthetical explanations makes contracts accessible to non-lawyers while maintaining legal rigor.

Enter your email to unlock this prompt

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Act as a business lawyer. Draft an independent contractor agreement in plain English.

**Engagement:**
- Company: [YOUR BUSINESS, LOCATION]
- Contractor: [NAME OR TBD]
- Project: [WHAT THEY WILL DO]
- Deliverables: [SPECIFIC ITEMS]
- Timeline: [START, END, MILESTONES]
- Payment: [AMOUNT/RATE, SCHEDULE, METHOD]

**Special Terms:**
- IP ownership: [WHO OWNS THE WORK]
- Confidentiality: [WHAT THEY WILL ACCESS]
- Non-compete: [ANY RESTRICTIONS]
- Revisions: [HOW MANY INCLUDED]
- Tools/expenses: [WHO PROVIDES/PAYS]

**Include sections for:**
1. Parties and date
2. Scope of work with deliverables
3. Compensation and invoicing
4. Independent contractor status (not employee)
5. IP ownership with pre-existing IP carve-out
6. Confidentiality (2-year duration)
7. Revision process
8. Termination and notice period
9. Liability limitation
10. Dispute resolution
11. General provisions
12. Signature blocks

Plain English. Explain legal terms in parentheses. End with AI disclaimer.
Notes: ChatGPT handles contract generation well. Use a Custom GPT with your standard terms pre-loaded for consistent agreements.

I need an independent contractor agreement drafted in plain English.

**Details:**
- My business: [NAME, LOCATION]
- Contractor: [NAME OR TBD]
- What they will do: [PROJECT DESCRIPTION]
- Deliverables: [SPECIFIC ITEMS]
- Timeline: [START, END, MILESTONES]
- Payment: [AMOUNT, SCHEDULE, METHOD]
- IP ownership: [WHO OWNS THE WORK]
- Confidentiality: [WHAT THEY WILL ACCESS]
- Non-compete: [ANY RESTRICTIONS]
- Revisions included: [NUMBER]
- Tools/expenses: [WHO PROVIDES]

**Include:** Parties, scope, compensation, contractor status, IP, confidentiality, revisions, termination, liability, dispute resolution, general provisions, signatures.

Write in plain English with legal terms explained in parentheses. End with an AI disclaimer recommending attorney review.
Notes: Gemini can research state-specific contractor classification rules to flag compliance risks.

Enter your email to unlock this prompt

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Help me write a contract for hiring a freelancer or contractor.

**The job:**
- My business: [NAME, LOCATION]
- Contractor: [NAME OR "to be determined"]
- What they will do: [DESCRIBE THE PROJECT]
- What I expect to receive: [LIST DELIVERABLES]
- When: [START DATE, END DATE, KEY DEADLINES]
- Pay: [HOW MUCH, HOW OFTEN, HOW THEY INVOICE]

**Other important stuff:**
- Who owns the work they create: [ME OR THEM]
- What confidential info they will see: [DESCRIBE]
- Can they work for my competitors: [YES/NO/WITH LIMITS]
- How many rounds of revisions: [NUMBER]
- Who provides tools and pays expenses: [ME OR THEM]

**Write a complete agreement with:** who the parties are, what the work is, how they get paid, that they are a contractor not an employee, who owns the work, confidentiality rules, revision process, how to end the agreement, liability limits, how to handle disagreements, and signature lines.

Use simple language. If you need a legal term, explain what it means. End with a note saying this was AI-generated and should be reviewed by a lawyer.
Notes: Copilot in Word can format this directly as a professional contract document.
